item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know everyone already said it, but the removal of the logo is just such a nice touch :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is glorious! This makes SRAs more compelling as opposed to them merely being some kind of null field generator. Plus all the 1s; this is my headcanon now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So that’s what a Scranton Reality Anchor looks like… nice.

In all seriousness, I love the idea and I think it’s executed well. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a really interesting idea and it is short (fluid) also there wasn't lack of creativity (data redacted,data expunged etc.) Best thing about this SCP was the simple idea behind it. Overall i would give it 10/10 [+1]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No vote.

I can't dispute that it's well written and goes above and beyond to immerse the reader in the idea that an SRA is just placebo tech propagated by memetic agents; that means it has a place and should stay. But it feels rather a lot like just taking the piss out of something for the sake of it, which I'm not fond of as an impulse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's deliberate. Reread the file, specifically in regards to SCP-4065-3.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I’ll give this a plus one, because though I do like SRAs when used as a temporary method, they are just annoying sometimes. Well done.

Also

why is the logo missing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the concept behind this post, and that's why it's held my +1 for so long, but upon rereading I'm just now realizing the sheer number of semicolons used. Usually it's not something I'd care about, but nine semicolons in six paragraphs is pretty egregious in my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i like to imagine that every other scp that's contained with reality anchors or that measures humes is Just Bullshit.

And thus, my dreams made real.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find the article too short! It seems like this would have major ramifications. I really like the idea but feel it's underdeveloped; neutral vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I gotta say, this one is incredible. you managed to overthrow my view of the SCP universe with a 2-minute read - the only others that have done that are big, novella-length SCP-001s. plus, there's a boatload of fridge brilliance. enjoy my +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
reached the second sentence of the description and am placing a provisional +1

edit: UPHELD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy crap, the idea that SRAs are just a super effective Placebo is the coolest thing I've ever heard

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a real fun interpretation of SRAs that stays interesting without solely being "screw SRAs," particularly with the idea that this can work for containment by just tricking reality benders into thinking they can't manipulate reality.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Simple, clever, and important without being obnoxious about it. It's one of the best kinds of article. Removing the header logo (which I initially assumed was just a technical issue on my end) is icing on this very satisfying cake. The only thing that I wanted from the article was more, but I personally don't know how to expand on this any further than you already have.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's what cinched the upvote for me.

Could you say it sealed the deal? 😎

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Huh. Pretty neat.
*notices the SCP logo is missing from the header*
Oh. Oh no.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like how you removed the Foundation logo from the top of the page. That's what cinched the upvote for me.

This idea would actually be fairly easy to test in-universe though. You just get a non-sapient reality-bender that can't be infected by the meme and see if the Scranton Reality Anchor has any effect.

In my headcanon, this article is just deliberate disinformation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Normally with articles that are essentially just shitting on an established piece of lore1, I tend to get bored and downvote. However, the You Are Not Immune to Propaganda aspect was rather well done, so I'm perfectly fine upvoting this.

Footnotes
1. Provided the lore isn't something batshit stupid like monthly terminations or skeleton keycards.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i don't like humes or SRAs, and people usually don't use them consistently anyways, so why not write something about why that is? scranton is a woman this time because I Do What I Want. you can see this as an alternate universe—it's definitely not canon with SCP-3001 or SCP-3326—but i like to imagine that every other scp that's contained with reality anchors or that measures humes is Just Bullshit.

thank u Weryllium and DolphinSlugchugger for the critique

oh and pls no declassify thx

image credit
– hide block

Memetic inoculation thingy CC-BY-SA 3.0 from wikimedia commons, edited by me, original creator Irina Pechkarjova.
SCP-4065-3 instances used in memetic inoculation thingy from Dr Mackenzie's Sketchbook; the quote in the memetic inoculation thingy is from David Hume's Treatise on Human Nature, and that guy's been dead for almost 250 years so it's public domain.
SCP-4065-1 public domain from Wikimedia Commons, edited by me
